pattern: The removal amounts are fixed in advance no matter what anyone chooses, so the whole game is really just asking which counted-down turn is the first one the pile can't afford.
hints: Neither player ever has a real choice -- the amount removed on turn t is always 10, 9, 8, ... regardless of who is moving, so you can simulate the whole game without any decision-making.
Keep subtracting the next required amount from the pile and alternate whose turn it is; stop as soon as the pile has less than the required amount.
Whoever's turn it is when the pile can no longer afford the required amount is the loser -- so Alice wins exactly when that turn belongs to Bob.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, Alice cannot even move
n exactly 10, Alice empties it exactly
one less than the full countdown sum
n just below what Alice needs for her first move
